get.description:
Query inference logs.
* Without timestamps (from/to) returns last `limit` messages (in last month).
* With `from` only, returns first `limit` messages after `from` (inclusive).
* With `to` only, returns last `limit` messages before `to` (inclusive).
* With both `from` and `to`, return the first `limit` messages after `from`, but not later than `to`.
* `from` and `to` should be no more than a month apart.

parameters.name: deploy_id

schema.description: the deploy id to get the logs from
schema.title: Deploy Id

parameters.description: the deploy id to get the logs from

parameters.name: from

schema.description: start of period, in fractional seconds since unix epoch (inclusive)
schema.title: From

parameters.description: start of period, in fractional seconds since unix epoch (inclusive)

parameters.name: to

schema.description: end of period, in fractional seconds since unix epoch (exclusive)
schema.title: To

parameters.description: end of period, in fractional seconds since unix epoch (exclusive)

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: how many items to return at most (default 100, in [1, 1000])
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: how many items to return at most (default 100, in [1, 1000])

public.title: Public
public.description: whether to make the model public of private

input_file_id.title: Input File Id
input_file_id.description: The ID of an uploaded file that contains requests for the new batch.

endpoint.title: Endpoint
endpoint.description: The endpoint to be used for all requests in the batch. Currently /v1/chat/completions, /v1/completions are supported.

completion_window.title: Completion Window
completion_window.description: The time frame within which the batch should be processed. Currently only 24h is supported.

metadata.title: Metadata
metadata.description: Optional metadata to be stored with the batch.

messages.title: Messages
messages.description: conversation messages: (user,assistant,tool)*,user including one system message anywhere

stream.title: Stream
stream.description: whether to stream the output via SSE or return the full response

temperature.title: Temperature
temperature.description: What sampling temperature to use, between 0 and 2. Higher values like 0.8 will make the output more random, while lower values like 0.2 will make it more focused and deterministic

top_p.title: Top P
top_p.description: An alternative to sampling with temperature, called nucleus sampling, where the model considers the results of the tokens with top_p probability mass. So 0.1 means only the tokens comprising the top 10% probability mass are considered.

min_p.title: Min P
min_p.description: Float that represents the minimum probability for a token to be considered, relative to the probability of the most likely token. Must be in [0, 1]. Set to 0 to disable this.

top_k.title: Top K
top_k.description: Sample from the best k (number of) tokens. 0 means off

max_tokens.title: Max Tokens
max_tokens.description:
The maximum number of tokens to generate in the chat completion.

The total length of input tokens and generated tokens is limited by the model's context length. If explicitly set to None it will be the model's max context length minus input length or 16384, whichever is smaller.

stop.title: Stop
stop.description: up to 16 sequences where the API will stop generating further tokens

presence_penalty.title: Presence Penalty
presence_penalty.description: Positive values penalize new tokens based on whether they appear in the text so far, increasing the model's likelihood to talk about new topics.

frequency_penalty.title: Frequency Penalty
frequency_penalty.description: Positive values penalize new tokens based on how many times they appear in the text so far, increasing the model's likelihood to talk about new topics.

tools.title: Tools
tools.description: A list of tools the model may call. Currently, only functions are supported as a tool.

tool_choice.title: Tool Choice
tool_choice.description: Controls which (if any) function is called by the model. none means the model will not call a function and instead generates a message. auto means the model can pick between generating a message or calling a function. required means the model must call a function. defined tool means the model must call that specific tool. none is the default when no functions are present. auto is the default if functions are present.

response_format.title: Response Format
response_format.description: The format of the response. Currently, only json is supported.

repetition_penalty.title: Repetition Penalty
repetition_penalty.description: Alternative penalty for repetition, but multiplicative instead of additive (> 1 penalize, < 1 encourage)

user.title: User
user.description: A unique identifier representing your end-user, which can help monitor and detect abuse. Avoid sending us any identifying information. We recommend hashing user identifiers.

seed.title: Seed
seed.description: Seed for random number generator. If not provided, a random seed is used. Determinism is not guaranteed.

logprobs.title: Logprobs
logprobs.description: Whether to return log probabilities of the output tokens or not.If true, returns the log probabilities of each output token returned in the `content` of `message`.

reasoning_effort.title: Reasoning Effort
reasoning_effort.description: Constrains effort on reasoning for reasoning models. Currently supported values are none, low, medium, and high. Reducing reasoning effort can result in faster responses and fewer tokens used on reasoning in a response. Setting to none disables reasoning entirely if the model supports.

prompt_cache_key.title: Prompt Cache Key
prompt_cache_key.description: A key to identify prompt cache for reuse across requests. If provided, the prompt will be cached and can be reused in subsequent requests with the same key.

prompt.title: Prompt
prompt.description: input prompt - a single string is currently supported

max_tokens.title: Max Tokens
max_tokens.description:
The maximum number of tokens to generate in the completion.

The total length of input tokens and generated tokens is limited by the model's context length.If explicitly set to None it will be the model's max context length minus input length or 16384, whichever is smaller.

temperature.title: Temperature
temperature.description: What sampling temperature to use, between 0 and 2. Higher values like 0.8 will make the output more random, while lower values like 0.2 will make it more focused and deterministic

top_p.title: Top P
top_p.description: An alternative to sampling with temperature, called nucleus sampling, where the model considers the results of the tokens with top_p probability mass. So 0.1 means only the tokens comprising the top 10% probability mass are considered.

min_p.title: Min P
min_p.description: Float that represents the minimum probability for a token to be considered, relative to the probability of the most likely token. Must be in [0, 1]. Set to 0 to disable this.

top_k.title: Top K
top_k.description: Sample from the best k (number of) tokens. 0 means off

stream.title: Stream
stream.description: whether to stream the output via SSE or return the full response

logprobs.title: Logprobs
logprobs.description: return top tokens and their log-probabilities

echo.title: Echo
echo.description: return prompt as part of the respons

stop.title: Stop
stop.description: up to 16 sequences where the API will stop generating further tokens

presence_penalty.title: Presence Penalty
presence_penalty.description: Positive values penalize new tokens based on whether they appear in the text so far, increasing the model's likelihood to talk about new topics.

frequency_penalty.title: Frequency Penalty
frequency_penalty.description: Positive values penalize new tokens based on how many times they appear in the text so far, increasing the model's likelihood to talk about new topics.

response_format.title: Response Format
response_format.description: The format of the response. Currently, only json is supported.

repetition_penalty.title: Repetition Penalty
repetition_penalty.description: Alternative penalty for repetition, but multiplicative instead of additive (> 1 penalize, < 1 encourage)

user.title: User
user.description: A unique identifier representing your end-user, which can help  monitor and detect abuse. Avoid sending us any identifying information. We recommend hashing user identifiers.

seed.title: Seed
seed.description: Seed for random number generator. If not provided, a random seed is used. Determinism is not guaranteed.

stop_token_ids.title: Stop Token Ids
stop_token_ids.description: List of token IDs that will stop generation when encountered

prompt_cache_key.title: Prompt Cache Key
prompt_cache_key.description: A key to identify prompt cache for reuse across requests. If provided, the prompt will be cached and can be reused in subsequent requests with the same key.

data.description: Optional multi-modal data to pass alongside the prompt. Only supported for a small number of non-chat-native vision models. Images must be base64 data URIs (e.g. 'data:image/png;base64,...').

service_tier.description: The service tier used for processing the request. When set to 'priority', the request will be processed with higher priority (only applies to models that support it).

dimensions.title: Dimensions
dimensions.description: The number of dimensions in the embedding. If not provided, the model's default will be used.If provided bigger than model's default, the embedding will be padded with zeros.

revised_prompt.title: Revised Prompt
revised_prompt.description: The prompt used to generate this image

url.title: Url
url.description: The URL of the generated image

n.title: N
n.description: The number of images to generate.

response_format.description: The format in which the generated images are returned. Currently only b64_json is supported.

size.title: Size
size.description: The size of the generated images. Available sizes depend on the model.

user.title: User
user.description: A unique identifier representing your end-user, which can help to monitor and detect abuse.

image.title: Image
image.description: Input image bytes for editing task

prompt.title: Prompt
prompt.description: A text description of the desired image edits.
examples: Add a hat to the cat

mask.title: Mask
mask.description: An additional image whose fully transparent areas (e.g. where alpha is zero) indicate where image should be edited. Must be a valid PNG file, less than 4MB, and have the same dimensions as image.

model.title: Model
model.description: The model to use for image generation.

n.title: N
n.description: The number of images to generate.

response_format.description: The format in which the generated images are returned. Currently only b64_json is supported.

size.title: Size
size.description: The size of the generated images. Available sizes depend on the model.

examples: A photo of an astronaut riding a horse on Mars.

quality.title: Quality

style.title: Style

created.title: Created

n.title: N

response_format.description: The format in which the generated images are returned. Currently only b64_json is supported.

size.title: Size

user.title: User
user.description: A unique identifier representing your end-user, which can help to monitor and detect abuse.

image.title: Image

service_tier.description: The service tier used for processing the request. When set to 'priority', the request will be processed with higher priority (only applies to models that support it).

input.title: Input
examples:
I'm beginnin' to feel like a Rap God, Rap God
All my people from the front to the back nod, back nod

voice.title: Voice

extra_body.title: Extra Body
extra_body.description: Extra body parameters for the model.

max_tokens.title: Max Tokens
max_tokens.description: The maximum context size of this model, if applicable

mf_description.title: Mf Description
mf_description.description: Model family description for this section, if applicable. Will use description from the section if not set

rate_limit.title: Rate Limit
rate_limit.description: Per model outstanding request rate limit

tpm_rate_limit.title: Tpm Rate Limit
tpm_rate_limit.description: Per model token per minute rate limit

regex.title: Regex
regex.description: Regex pattern for structured output when type is 'regex'

min_instances.title: Min Instances
min_instances.description: Minimum number of model instances to run

max_instances.title: Max Instances
max_instances.description: Maximum number of model instances to run

models.title: Models
models.description: allow inference only to the specified model names

expires_delta.title: Expires Delta
expires_delta.description: how many seconds in the future should the token be valid for

expires_at.title: Expires At
